title_en: 5. Would you like to meet him outside the institution? (cont.)
content_en: Any idea how he delivered that lecture? Did he speak? How could he, for he was dumb! Did he use sign language? How could he, for he was blind and had never learnt sign language! He delivered his lecture by 'touch' language. Abu 'Abdullah, who was the translator, placed his hand be76 The tongue is king tween Fayiz's hands. Fayiz would then touch his hand in particular ways by which Abu 'Abdullah knew what Fayiz was trying to say. He would then relate to us what he understood from Fayiz. Sometimes it would take up to a quarter of an hour to understand what he was trying to say. Fayiz would then sit still, not knowing whether the translator had ﬁnished translating or not, as he was of course neither able to hear nor see. When the translator would ﬁnish speaking, he would strike Fayiz's knee once again. Fayiz would then stretch out his hands and grab hold of the translator's hand and touch it in particular ways to convey his message. The people remained seated, watching Fayiz and the translator communicating with amazement. Fayiz was encouraging the audience to repent to Allah for their sins. He would at times hold his ears, then his tongue, and then place his hands over his eyes. We would not understand what he meant until Abu 'Abdullah translated for us. He was advising the people to guard their ears and eyes lest they fall into the forbidden. As I looked at the people, I noticed some people sigh, "SubhanAllah", others whispered to the person sitting next to them, others were watching with great interest and still others were crying. As for me, mind had wandered off very far indeed. I began to contemplate on the faculties Fayiz had in comparison to what the audience were blessed with, and contrasted that with Fayiz's services for the religion in comparison to that of the audience. Surely the concern that this blind, deaf and dumb person had for Islam was greater than the concern in the hearts of all the audience combined! The tongue is king Although the man had a very limited number of faculties, he was exhausting himself in service to this religion. He felt as if he was a soldier of Islam, responsible for anyone who sinned or fell short. He was constantly moving his hand, as if he was admonishing, "For how much longer will you abandon the prayer? For how much longer will you continue to look at the Haram? For how much longer will you continue with your disgraceful acts? For how much longer will you continue to consume Haram? For how much longer will you continue upon Shirk? For how much longer? Is it not enough that its enemies are waging a war against our religion? Why do you have to join the war against our religion?" The poor man's complexion would often change as he shared what he had in his heart with the audience, who were in turn were greatly affected. Although I did not turn around to look at the people, I could still hear people crying and sighing "SubhanAllah!" Fayiz eventually ﬁnished his lecture and got up, as Abu 'Abdullah held his hand. The audience came to him to greet him. I saw him greeting them and it was obvious to me that he treated everyone equally. He greeted everyone and did not distinguish between rich and poor, leaders and followers. I thought to myself that if only all people were like Fayiz! Abu 'Abdullah took Fayiz out of the mosque. I walked by their side as they made their way to the car. I saw Fayiz and the translator joking with each other and thought to myself how insigniﬁcant this world was! How many people have not been afﬂicted with a quarter of Fayiz's problems, and yet they are still The tongue is king unable to overcome their sadness and sorrow! How about those who have chronic illnesses, such as kidney failure, paralysis, thrombus, diabetes or other impediments? Why don't they enjoy their lives and learn to live with the reality? How wonderful it is when Allah afﬂicts His servant and then looks into his heart to ﬁnd that he is still thankful, pleased and yearning for his reward from Him! Many days have passed, and yet Fayiz's picture is still embedded in my memory. If Fayiz can be so successful in life and earn the love of people being blind, dumb and deaf, then how about the one who has been blessed with a tongue, sight and hearing? Use your tongue to gain people's love! Reality... A man's ﬂesh is not ﬁt for eating nor is his skin ﬁt for clothing − there is nothing in him except the sweetness of his tongue. The tongue is king (77) Control your tongue A person may utter a callus word that displeases Allah for which He will be angry with him until the day He meets him. This is why the Prophet warned people from uttering something callously without contemplating its consequences.
content_id: Adakah yang tahu bagaimana ia menyampaikan ceramah itu? Apakah ia berbicara? Bagaimana mungkin, karena ia bisu! Apakah ia menggunakan bahasa isyarat? Bagaimana mungkin, karena ia buta dan tidak pernah belajar bahasa isyarat! Ia menyampaikan ceramahnya dengan bahasa sentuhan. Abu Abdullah, yang bertugas sebagai penerjemah, meletakkan tangannya di antara kedua tangan Fayiz. Fayiz kemudian menyentuh tangannya dengan cara-cara tertentu yang membuat Abu Abdullah mengerti apa yang ingin disampaikan Fayiz. Ia kemudian menyampaikan kepada kami apa yang ia pahami dari Fayiz. Terkadang memerlukan waktu hingga seperempat jam untuk memahami apa yang ia coba sampaikan. Fayiz kemudian duduk diam, tidak mengetahui apakah sang penerjemah sudah selesai menerjemahkan atau belum, karena tentu saja ia tidak bisa mendengar maupun melihat. Ketika penerjemah selesai berbicara, ia mengetuk lutut Fayiz sekali lagi. Fayiz kemudian mengulurkan tangannya dan meraih tangan sang penerjemah serta menyentuhnya dengan cara-cara tertentu untuk menyampaikan pesannya. Orang-orang tetap duduk, memandang Fayiz dan penerjemah berkomunikasi dengan penuh kekaguman. Fayiz sedang mendorong hadirin untuk bertaubat kepada Allah atas dosa-dosa mereka. Sesekali ia memegang telinganya, lalu lisannya, kemudian meletakkan kedua tangannya di atas matanya. Kami tidak memahami maksudnya sampai Abu Abdullah menerjemahkan untuk kami. Ia menasihati orang-orang untuk menjaga telinga dan mata mereka agar tidak terjerumus ke dalam yang diharamkan. Saat aku memandang orang-orang, aku melihat sebagian mereka menghela napas, "SubhanAllah", sebagian lain berbisik kepada orang yang duduk di sebelah mereka, sebagian lagi memandang dengan penuh minat, dan sebagian lainnya menangis. Adapun aku sendiri, pikiranku telah melanglang jauh. Aku mulai merenungkan kemampuan yang dimiliki Fayiz dibandingkan dengan apa yang dimiliki hadirin, dan membandingkan pengabdian Fayiz terhadap agama dengan pengabdian hadirin. Sungguh, kepedulian orang yang buta, tuli, dan bisu ini terhadap Islam lebih besar daripada kepedulian seluruh hadirin yang ada di sana! Meskipun orang ini memiliki kemampuan yang sangat terbatas, ia menguras dirinya dalam mengabdi kepada agama ini. Ia merasa seolah-olah dirinya adalah seorang prajurit Islam yang bertanggung jawab atas setiap orang yang berbuat dosa atau lalai. Ia terus-menerus menggerakkan tangannya, seolah-olah sedang menasihati, "Sampai kapan kamu akan meninggalkan salat? Sampai kapan kamu akan terus memandang yang haram? Sampai kapan kamu akan terus melakukan perbuatan memalukan? Sampai kapan kamu akan terus mengonsumsi yang haram? Sampai kapan kamu akan terus berada dalam kemusyrikan? Sampai kapan? Tidakkah cukup bahwa musuh-musuhnya sedang memerangi agama kita? Mengapa kamu harus ikut memerangi agama kita?" Air muka lelaki malang itu sering berubah saat ia berbagi isi hatinya dengan hadirin, yang pada gilirannya sangat tersentuh. Meskipun aku tidak berbalik untuk melihat orang-orang, aku masih bisa mendengar suara tangis dan desahan "SubhanAllah!" Fayiz akhirnya menyelesaikan ceramahnya dan berdiri, sementara Abu Abdullah memegang tangannya. Hadirin datang untuk menyapanya. Aku melihatnya menyambut mereka dan jelas bagiku bahwa ia memperlakukan semua orang secara setara. Ia menyambut semua orang dan tidak membedakan antara kaya dan miskin, pemimpin dan pengikut. Aku berkata dalam hati, andai saja semua orang seperti Fayiz! Abu Abdullah membawa Fayiz keluar dari masjid. Aku berjalan di sisi mereka saat mereka menuju ke mobil. Aku melihat Fayiz dan sang penerjemah bercanda satu sama lain dan aku pun berpikir betapa tidak berartinya dunia ini! Berapa banyak orang yang tidak ditimpa seperempat dari masalah Fayiz, namun mereka masih tidak mampu mengatasi kesedihan dan duka mereka! Bagaimana dengan mereka yang menderita penyakit kronis, seperti gagal ginjal, kelumpuhan, trombosis, diabetes, atau hambatan lainnya? Mengapa mereka tidak menikmati hidup mereka dan belajar untuk hidup dengan kenyataan? Betapa indahnya ketika Allah menguji hamba-Nya dan kemudian melihat ke dalam hatinya untuk mendapati bahwa ia masih bersyukur, ridha, dan merindukan pahala dari-Nya! Banyak hari telah berlalu, namun gambaran tentang Fayiz masih tertanam dalam ingatanku. Jika Fayiz bisa begitu sukses dalam kehidupan dan mendapatkan cinta orang-orang meskipun buta, bisu, dan tuli, bagaimana dengan orang yang telah dikaruniai lisan, penglihatan, dan pendengaran? Gunakanlah lisanmu untuk meraih cinta orang-orang! Kenyataan... Daging seorang manusia tidak layak untuk dimakan dan kulitnya tidak layak untuk dijadikan pakaian — tidak ada yang ada padanya kecuali manisnya lisannya. Lisan adalah raja. (77) Kendalikan lisanmu. Seseorang mungkin mengucapkan kata-kata tidak bermakna yang membuat Allah murka, sehingga Dia murka kepadanya hingga hari ia bertemu dengan-Nya. Itulah mengapa Nabi memperingatkan orang-orang dari mengucapkan sesuatu sembarangan tanpa merenungkan konsekuensinya.
content_ar: يختتم المؤلف كتابه "استمتع بحياتك" بمزيد من المواقف والحكايات المُستقاة من السيرة النبوية الشريفة وسير الصحابة الكرام. ويؤكد أهمية حسن الخلق والتواضع والإخلاص في التعامل مع الناس، مُختتمًا بنصائح عملية لمن يريد أن يعيش حياةً سعيدة مع نفسه ومع مجتمعه.
